Setting the `create_before_destroy` lifecycle Meta-argument to `true` is highly recommended if modifying the `included_keyword_configuration` field to avoid unexpectedly disabling Sensitive Data Scanner groups.

disabled.description: Whether the service account is disabled. Defaults to `false`.

name.description: Name for the service account.

roles.description: A list of role IDs to assign to the service account.

block.description: Provides a Datadog service account resource. This can be used to create and manage Datadog service accounts.

created_at.description: Creation date of the application key.

key.description: The value of the service account application key. This value cannot be imported.

last4.description: The last four characters of the application key.

name.description: Name of the application key.

scopes.description: Authorization scopes for the Application Key. Application Keys configured with no scopes have full access.

service_account_id.description: ID of the service account that owns this key.

block.description: Provides a Datadog `service_account_application_key` resource. This can be used to create and manage Datadog service account application keys.

service_definition.description: The YAML/JSON formatted definition of the service

block.description: Provides a Datadog service definition resource. This can be used to create and manage Datadog service definitions in the service catalog using the YAML/JSON definition.

description.description: A description of this service level objective.

force_delete.description: A boolean indicating whether this monitor can be deleted even if it's referenced by other resources (for example, dashboards).

groups.description: A static set of groups to filter monitor-based SLOs

monitor_ids.description: A static set of monitor IDs to use as part of the SLO

name.description: Name of Datadog service level objective

tags.description: A list of tags to associate with your service level objective. This can help you categorize and filter service level objectives in the service level objectives page of the UI. **Note**: it's not currently possible to filter by these tags when querying via the API. If default tags are present at the provider level, they will be added to this resource.

target_threshold.description: The objective's target in `(0,100)`. This must match the corresponding thresholds of the primary time frame.

timeframe.description: The primary time frame for the objective. The mapping from these types to the types found in the Datadog Web UI can be found in the Datadog API documentation page. Valid values are `7d`, `30d`, `90d`, `custom`.

type.description: The type of the service level objective. The mapping from these types to the types found in the Datadog Web UI can be found in the Datadog API [documentation page](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/v1/service-level-objectives/#create-a-slo-object). Valid values are `metric`, `monitor`, `time_slice`.

validate.description: Whether or not to validate the SLO. It checks if monitors added to a monitor SLO already exist.

warning_threshold.description: The objective's warning value in `(0,100)`. This must be greater than the target value and match the corresponding thresholds of the primary time frame.

numerator.description: The sum of all the `good` events.

block.description: The metric query of good / total events. Use this for metric SLOs as an alternative to `sli_specification`.

good_events_formula.description: The formula that specifies how to compute the good events.

total_events_formula.description: The formula that specifies how to compute the total events.

data_source.description: The data source for metrics queries. Defaults to `"metrics"`.

name.description: The name of the query for use in formulas.

block.description: A timeseries formula and functions metrics query.
A list of data-source-specific queries that are referenced in the formulas.
A count-based (metric) SLI specification. Composed of a good events formula, a total events formula, and the underlying metric queries.

comparator.description: The comparator used to compare the SLI value to the threshold. Valid values are `>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=`.

query_interval_seconds.description: The interval used when querying data, which defines the size of a time slice. Valid values are `60`, `300`. Defaults to `300`.

threshold.description: The threshold value to which each SLI value will be compared.

formula_expression.description: The formula string, which is an expression involving named queries.

block.description: A list that contains exactly one formula, as only a single formula may be used to define a timeseries query for a time-slice SLO.

data_source.description: The data source for metrics queries. Defaults to `"metrics"`.

name.description: The name of the query for use in formulas.

block.description: A timeseries formula and functions metrics query.
A list of data-source-specific queries that are in the formula.
A timeseries query, containing named data-source-specific queries and a formula involving the named queries.
The time slice condition, composed of 3 parts: 1. The timeseries query, 2. The comparator, and 3. The threshold. Optionally, a fourth part, the query interval, can be provided.
A generic SLI specification. This is used for both time-slice SLOs and count-based (metric) SLOs.

target.description: The objective's target in `(0,100)`.

target_display.description: A string representation of the target that indicates its precision. It uses trailing zeros to show significant decimal places (e.g. `98.00`).

timeframe.description: The time frame for the objective. The mapping from these types to the types found in the Datadog Web UI can be found in the Datadog API documentation page. Valid values are `7d`, `30d`, `90d`, `custom`.

warning.description: The objective's warning value in `(0,100)`. This must be greater than the target value.

warning_display.description: A string representation of the warning target (see the description of the target_display field for details).

block.description: A list of thresholds and targets that define the service level objectives from the provided SLIs.
Provides a Datadog service level objective resource. This can be used to create and manage Datadog service level objectives.

category.description: Category the SLO correction belongs to. Valid values are `Scheduled Maintenance`, `Outside Business Hours`, `Deployment`, `Other`.

description.description: Description of the correction being made.

duration.description: Length of time in seconds for a specified `rrule` recurring SLO correction (required if specifying `rrule`)

end.description: Ending time of the correction in epoch seconds. Required for one time corrections, but optional if `rrule` is specified

rrule.description: Recurrence rules as defined in the iCalendar RFC 5545. Supported rules for SLO corrections are `FREQ`, `INTERVAL`, `COUNT` and `UNTIL`.

slo_id.description: ID of the SLO that this correction will be applied to.

start.description: Starting time of the correction in epoch seconds.

timezone.description: The timezone to display in the UI for the correction times. Prefers IANA timezone name format (for example, 'America/Los_Angeles', 'Europe/Paris'), but some common standard abbreviations are supported. Defaults to 'UTC'.

block.description: Resource for interacting with the slo_correction API.

entity.description: The catalog entity definition. Entity must be a valid entity YAML/JSON structure.

block.description: Provides a Datadog Software Catalog Entity resource. This can be used to create and manage entities in Datadog Software Catalog using the YAML/JSON definition.

name.description: The name of the span-based metric. This field can't be updated after creation.

aggregation_type.description: The type of aggregation to use. This field can't be updated after creation.

include_percentiles.description: Toggle to include or exclude percentile aggregations for distribution metrics. Only present when the `aggregation_type` is `distribution`.

path.description: The path to the value the span-based metric will aggregate on (only used if the aggregation type is a "distribution"). This field can't be updated after creation.

query.description: The search query - following the span search syntax. Defaults to `"*"`.

path.description: The path to the value the span-based metric will be aggregated over.

tag_name.description: Eventual name of the tag that gets created. By default, the path attribute is used as the tag name.

block.description: Provides a Datadog SpansMetric resource. This can be used to create and manage Datadog spans_metric.

on_demand_concurrency_cap.description: Value of the on-demand concurrency cap, customizing the number of Synthetic tests run in parallel. Value must be at least 1.

block.description: Provides a Datadog Synthetics On Demand Concurrency Cap API resource. This can be used to manage the Concurrency Cap for Synthetic tests.

description.description: Description of the global variable. Defaults to `""`.

is_fido.description: If set to true, the global variable is a FIDO variable. Defaults to `false`.

is_totp.description: If set to true, the global variable is a TOTP variable. Defaults to `false`.

name.description: Synthetics global variable name. Must be all uppercase with underscores.

parse_test_id.description: Id of the Synthetics test to use for a variable from test.

restricted_roles.description: A list of role identifiers to associate with the Synthetics global variable. **Deprecated.** This field is no longer supported by the Datadog API. Please use `datadog_restriction_policy` instead.

secure.description: If set to true, the value of the global variable is hidden. This setting is automatically set to `true` if `is_totp` or `is_fido` is set to `true`. Defaults to `false`.

tags.description: A list of tags to associate with your synthetics global variable.

value.description: The value of the global variable. Required unless `is_fido` is set to `true` or `value_wo` is used

value_wo.description: Write-only value of the global variable. Must be used with `value_wo_version`.

value_wo_version.description: Version associated with the write-only value. Changing this triggers an update. Can be any string (e.g., '1', 'v2.1', '2024-Q1'). String length must be at least 1.

digits.description: Number of digits for the OTP. Value must be between 4 and 10.

refresh_interval.description: Interval for which to refresh the token (in seconds). Value must be between 0 and 999.

block.description: Parameters needed for MFA/TOTP.
Additional options for the variable, such as a MFA token.

field.description: Required when type = `http_header`. Defines the header to use to extract the value

local_variable_name.description: When type is `local_variable`, name of the local variable to use to extract the value.

type.description: Defines the source to use to extract the value. Valid values are `http_body`, `http_header`, `http_status_code`, `local_variable`.
Type of parser to extract the value. Valid values are `raw`, `json_path`, `regex`, `x_path`.

value.description: Value for the parser to use, required for type `json_path` or `regex`.

block.description: ID of the Synthetics test to use a source of the global variable value.
Provides a Datadog synthetics global variable resource. This can be used to create and manage Datadog synthetics global variables.

api_key.description: API key used to generate the private location configuration.

config.description: Configuration skeleton for the private location. See installation instructions of the private location on how to use this configuration.

description.description: Description of the private location. Defaults to `""`.

name.description: Synthetics private location name.

restriction_policy_resource_id.description: Resource ID to use when setting restrictions with a `datadog_restriction_policy` resource.

tags.description: A list of tags to associate with your synthetics private location.

restricted_roles.description: A set of role identifiers pulled from the Roles API to restrict read and write access. **Deprecated.** This field is no longer supported by the Datadog API. Please use `datadog_restriction_policy` instead.

block.description: The private location metadata
Provides a Datadog synthetics private location resource. This can be used to create and manage Datadog synthetics private locations.

name.description: Name of the Synthetics suite.

tags.description: A set of tags to associate with your synthetics suite.

alerting_threshold.description: Alerting threshold for the suite. Value must be between 0.000000 and 1.000000.

block.description: Options for the Synthetics suite.

alerting_criticality.description: Alerting criticality for the test. Valid values are `ignore`, `critical`.

block.description: List of tests in the Synthetics suite. Can be empty but the field is always sent to the API.
Provides a Datadog Synthetics Suite resource. This can be used to create and manage Synthetics test suites.

config_initial_application_arguments.description: Initial application arguments for the mobile test.

device_ids.description: Required if `type = "browser"`. Array with the different device IDs used to run the test.

force_delete_dependencies.description: A boolean indicating whether this synthetics test can be deleted even if it's referenced by other resources (for example, SLOs and composite monitors).

locations.description: Array of locations used to run the test. Refer to [the Datadog Synthetics location data source](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/DataDog/datadog/latest/docs/data-sources/synthetics_locations) to retrieve the list of locations or find the possible values listed in [this API response](https://app.datadoghq.com/api/v1/synthetics/locations?only_public=true).

message.description: A message to include with notifications for this synthetics test. Email notifications can be sent to specific users by using the same `@username` notation as events. Defaults to `""`.

monitor_id.description: ID of the monitor associated with the Datadog synthetics test.

name.description: Name of Datadog synthetics test.

request_metadata.description: Metadata to include when performing the gRPC request.

request_query.description: Query arguments name and value map.

set_cookie.description: Cookies to be used for a browser test request, using the [Set-Cookie](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Set-Cookie) syntax.

status.description: Define whether you want to start (`live`) or pause (`paused`) a Synthetic test. Valid values are `live`, `paused`.

subtype.description: The subtype for API or Network Path tests. For API tests, defaults to `http`. For Network Path tests, only `tcp`, `udp`, `icmp` are available. Valid values are `http`, `ssl`, `tcp`, `dns`, `multi`, `icmp`, `udp`, `websocket`, `grpc`.

tags.description: A list of tags to associate with your synthetics test. This can help you categorize and filter tests in the manage synthetics page of the UI. Default is an empty list (`[]`).

type.description: The type of Synthetics test. Valid values are `api`, `browser`, `mobile`, `network`.

variables_from_script.description: Variables defined from JavaScript code for API HTTP tests.

allow_failure.description: Determines whether or not to continue with test if this step fails.

exit_if_succeed.description: Determines whether or not to exit the test if the step succeeds.

is_critical.description: Determines whether or not to consider the entire test as failed if this step fails. Can be used only if `allow_failure` is `true`.

name.description: The name of the step.

request_metadata.description: Metadata to include when performing the gRPC request.

request_query.description: Query arguments name and value map.

subtest_public_id.description: Public ID of the test to be played as part of a `playSubTest` step type.

subtype.description: The subtype of the Synthetic multistep API test step. Valid values are `http`, `grpc`, `ssl`, `dns`, `tcp`, `udp`, `icmp`, `websocket`, `wait`, `playSubTest`. Defaults to `"http"`.

value.description: The time to wait in seconds. Minimum value: 0. Maximum value: 180.

code.description: If assertion type is `javascript`, this is the JavaScript code that performs the assertions.

operator.description: Assertion operator. **Note:** Only some combinations of `type` and `operator` are valid. Refer to `config.assertions` in the [Datadog API reference](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/synthetics/#create-an-api-test). Valid values are `contains`, `doesNotContain`, `is`, `isNot`, `lessThan`, `lessThanOrEqual`, `moreThan`, `moreThanOrEqual`, `matches`, `doesNotMatch`, `validates`, `isInMoreThan`, `isInLessThan`, `doesNotExist`, `isUndefined`, `validatesJSONPath`, `validatesJSONSchema`, `validatesXPath`, `md5`, `sha1`, `sha256`, `is`, `isNot`, `lessThan`, `lessThanOrEqual`, `moreThan`, `moreThanOrEqual`.

property.description: If assertion type is `header` or `grpcMetadata`, this is the header name. For other assertion types, this is an aggregation property: `avg`, `min`, `max`, or `stddev`.

target.description: Expected value. **Note:** Depends on the assertion type. Refer to `config.assertions` in the [Datadog API reference](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/synthetics/#create-an-api-test).

timings_scope.description: Timings scope for response time assertions. Valid values are `all`, `withoutDNS`.

type.description: Type of assertion. **Note:** Only some combinations of `type` and `operator` are valid. For API tests, refer to `config.assertions` in the [Datadog API reference](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/synthetics/#create-an-api-test). For Network Path tests, refer to `config.assertions` in the [Datadog API reference](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/synthetics/#create-a-network-path-test). Valid values are `body`, `header`, `statusCode`, `certificate`, `responseTime`, `property`, `recordEvery`, `recordSome`, `tlsVersion`, `minTlsVersion`, `latency`, `packetLossPercentage`, `packetsReceived`, `networkHop`, `receivedMessage`, `grpcHealthcheckStatus`, `grpcMetadata`, `grpcProto`, `connection`, `multiNetworkHop`, `jitter`, `bodyHash`, `javascript`.

elementsoperator.description: The element from the list of results to assert on. Select from `firstElementMatches` (the first element in the list), `everyElementMatches` (every element in the list), `atLeastOneElementMatches` (at least one element in the list), or `serializationMatches` (the serialized value of the list). Defaults to `"firstElementMatches"`.

operator.description: The specific operator to use on the path.

block.description: Expected structure if `operator` is `validatesJSONPath`. Exactly one nested block is allowed with the structure below.

jsonschema.description: The JSON Schema to validate the body against.

metaschema.description: The meta schema to use for the JSON Schema. Defaults to `"draft-07"`.

Assertions used for the test. Multiple `assertion` blocks are allowed with the structure below.

field.description: When type is `http_header` or `grpc_metadata`, name of the header or metadatum to extract.

secure.description: Determines whether or not the extracted value will be obfuscated.

type.description: Property of the Synthetics Test Response to use for the variable. Valid values are `grpc_message`, `grpc_metadata`, `http_body`, `http_header`, `http_status_code`.
Type of parser for a Synthetics global variable from a synthetics test. Valid values are `raw`, `json_path`, `regex`, `x_path`.

value.description: Regex or JSON path used for the parser. Not used with type `raw`.

block.description: Values to parse and save as variables from the response.

access_token_url.description: Access token url for `oauth-client` or `oauth-rop` authentication.

audience.description: Audience for `oauth-client` or `oauth-rop` authentication. Defaults to `""`.

resource.description: Resource for `oauth-client` or `oauth-rop` authentication. Defaults to `""`.

scope.description: Scope for `oauth-client` or `oauth-rop` authentication. Defaults to `""`.

token_api_authentication.description: Token API Authentication for `oauth-client` or `oauth-rop` authentication. Valid values are `header`, `body`.

type.description: Type of basic authentication to use when performing the test. Defaults to `"web"`.

block.description: The HTTP basic authentication credentials. Exactly one nested block is allowed with the structure below.

filename.description: File name for the certificate. Defaults to `"Provided in Terraform config"`.
File name for the certificate. Defaults to `"Provided in Terraform config"`.

block.description: Client certificate to use when performing the test request. Exactly one nested block is allowed with the structure below.

accept_self_signed.description: For SSL tests, whether or not the test should allow self signed certificates.

allow_insecure.description: Allows loading insecure content for a request in an API test or in a multistep API test step.

body_type.description: Type of the request body. Valid values are `text/plain`, `application/json`, `text/xml`, `text/html`, `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`, `graphql`, `application/octet-stream`, `multipart/form-data`.

call_type.description: The type of gRPC call to perform. Valid values are `healthcheck`, `unary`.

certificate_domains.description: By default, the client certificate is applied on the domain of the starting URL for browser tests. If you want your client certificate to be applied on other domains instead, add them in `certificate_domains`.

check_certificate_revocation.description: For SSL tests, whether or not the test should fail on revoked certificate in stapled OCSP.

destination_service.description: For Network Path tests, an optional label displayed for the destination host in the Network Path visualization.

disable_aia_intermediate_fetching.description: For SSL tests, whether or not the test should disable fetching intermediate certificates from AIA

dns_server.description: DNS server to use for DNS tests (`subtype = "dns"`).

dns_server_port.description: DNS server port to use for DNS tests.

e2e_queries.description: For Network Path tests, the number of packets sent to probe the destination to measure packet loss, latency, and jitter.

follow_redirects.description: Determines whether or not the API HTTP test should follow redirects.

form.description: Form data to be sent when `body_type` is `multipart/form-data`.

host.description: Host name to perform the test with.

http_version.description: HTTP version to use for an HTTP request in an API test or step. Valid values are `http1`, `http2`, `any`. Defaults to `"any"`.

is_message_base64_encoded.description: For Websocket tests, whether the message is treated as a base64-encoded string in the server.

max_ttl.description: For Network Path tests, the maximum time-to-live (max number of hops) used in outgoing probe packets.

message.description: For gRPC, UDP, and Websocket tests, message to send with the request.

method.description: Either the HTTP method/verb to use or a gRPC method available on the service set in the `service` field. Required if `subtype` is `HTTP` or if `subtype` is `grpc` and `callType` is `unary`.

no_saving_response_body.description: Determines whether or not to save the response body.

number_of_packets.description: Number of pings to use per test for ICMP tests (`subtype = "icmp"`) between 0 and 10.

plain_proto_file.description: The content of a proto file as a string.

port.description: Port to use when performing the test.

proto_json_descriptor.description: A protobuf JSON descriptor. **Deprecated.** Use `plain_proto_file` instead.

servername.description: For SSL tests, it specifies on which server you want to initiate the TLS handshake, allowing the server to present one of multiple possible certificates on the same IP address and TCP port number.

service.description: The gRPC service on which you want to perform the gRPC call.

should_track_hops.description: This will turn on a traceroute probe to discover all gateways along the path to the host destination. For ICMP tests (`subtype = "icmp"`).

source_service.description: For Network Path tests, an optional label displayed for the source host in the Network Path visualization

tcp_method.description: For TCP Network Path tests, the TCP traceroute strategy.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for the test.

traceroute_queries.description: For Network Path tests, the number of traceroute path tracings.

url.description: The URL to send the request to.

block.description: The request for the API step.

name.description: Name of the file.

type.description: Type of the file.

block.description: Files to be used as part of the request in the test.

url.description: URL of the proxy to perform the test.

block.description: The proxy to perform the test.

count.description: Number of retries needed to consider a location as failed before sending a notification alert. Maximum value: `3` for `api` tests, `2` for `browser` and `mobile` tests. Defaults to `0`.

interval.description: Interval between a failed test and the next retry in milliseconds. Maximum value: `5000`. Defaults to `300`.

allow_failure.description: Determines if the step should be allowed to fail.

always_execute.description: Determines whether or not to always execute this step even if the previous step failed or was skipped.

exit_if_succeed.description: Determines whether or not to exit the test if the step succeeds.

force_element_update.description: Force update of the "element" parameter for the step

is_critical.description: Determines whether or not to consider the entire test as failed if this step fails. Can be used only if `allow_failure` is `true`.

local_key.description: A unique identifier used to track steps after reordering.

name.description: Name of the step.

no_screenshot.description: Prevents saving screenshots of the step.

public_id.description: The identifier of the step on the backend.

timeout.description: Used to override the default timeout of a step.

type.description: Type of the step. Valid values are `assertCurrentUrl`, `assertElementAttribute`, `assertElementContent`, `assertElementPresent`, `assertEmail`, `assertFileDownload`, `assertFromJavascript`, `assertPageContains`, `assertPageLacks`, `assertRequests`, `click`, `extractFromJavascript`, `extractFromEmailBody`, `extractVariable`, `goToEmailLink`, `goToUrl`, `goToUrlAndMeasureTti`, `hover`, `playSubTest`, `pressKey`, `refresh`, `runApiTest`, `scroll`, `selectOption`, `typeText`, `uploadFiles`, `wait`.

append_to_content.description: Whether to append the `value` to existing text input content for a "typeText" step. By default, content is cleared before text input.

attribute.description: Name of the attribute to use for an "assert attribute" step.

check.description: Check type to use for an assertion step. Valid values are `equals`, `notEquals`, `contains`, `notContains`, `startsWith`, `notStartsWith`, `greater`, `lower`, `greaterEquals`, `lowerEquals`, `matchRegex`, `between`, `isEmpty`, `notIsEmpty`.

click_type.description: Type of click to use for a "click" step.

click_with_javascript.description: Whether to use `element.click()` for a "click" step. This is a more reliable way to interact with elements but does not emulate a real user interaction.

code.description: Javascript code to use for the step.

delay.description: Delay between each key stroke for a "type test" step.

element.description: Element to use for the step, JSON encoded string. Refer to the examples for a usage example showing the schema.

email.description: Details of the email for an "assert email" step, JSON encoded string.

file.description: JSON encoded string used for an "assert download" step. Refer to the examples for a usage example showing the schema.

files.description: Details of the files for an "upload files" step, JSON encoded string. Refer to the examples for a usage example showing the schema.

modifiers.description: Modifier to use for a "press key" step.

playing_tab_id.description: ID of the tab to play the subtest.

requests.description: Details of the requests for an "assert request" step, JSON encoded string. Refer to the examples for a usage example showing the schema.

subtest_public_id.description: ID of the Synthetics test to use as subtest.

enable_logs_sample.description: Whether or not a list of log values which triggered the alert is included. This is only used by log monitors.

enable_samples.description: Whether or not a list of samples which triggered the alert is included. This is only used by CI Test and Pipeline monitors.

escalation_message.description: Message included with a re-notification for this monitor.

evaluation_delay.description: Time (in seconds) for which evaluation is delayed. This is only used by metric monitors.

group_retention_duration.description: The time span after which groups with missing data are dropped from the monitor state. The minimum value is one hour, and the maximum value is 72 hours. Example values are: 60m, 1h, and 2d. This option is only available for APM Trace Analytics, Audit Trail, CI, Error Tracking, Event, Logs, and RUM monitors.

groupby_simple_monitor.description: Whether or not to trigger one alert if any source breaches a threshold.

include_tags.description: Whether or not notifications from the monitor automatically inserts its triggering tags into the title.

locked.description: Whether or not changes to the monitor are restricted to the creator or admins.

message.description: Message included with notifications for this monitor

monitor_tags_filter.description: A list of monitor tags to limit the search. This filters on the tags set on the monitor itself.

monitor_threshold_windows.description: Mapping containing `recovery_window` and `trigger_window` values, e.g. `last_15m`. This is only used by anomaly monitors.

name.description: Name of the monitor

name_filter.description: A monitor name to limit the search.

new_group_delay.description: Time (in seconds) to skip evaluations for new groups.

new_host_delay.description: Time (in seconds) allowing a host to boot and applications to fully start before starting the evaluation of monitor results.

no_data_timeframe.description: The number of minutes before the monitor notifies when data stops reporting.

notification_preset_name.description: Toggles the display of additional content sent in the monitor notification. Valid values are: `show_all`, `hide_query`, `hide_handles`, and `hide_all`.

notify_audit.description: Whether or not tagged users are notified on changes to the monitor.

notify_by.description: Controls what granularity a monitor alerts on. Only available for monitors with groupings. For instance, a monitor grouped by `cluster`, `namespace`, and `pod` can be configured to only notify on each new `cluster` violating the alert conditions by setting `notify_by` to `['cluster']`. Tags mentioned in `notify_by` must be a subset of the grouping tags in the query. For example, a query grouped by `cluster` and `namespace` cannot notify on `region`. Setting `notify_by` to `[*]` configures the monitor to notify as a simple-alert.

notify_no_data.description: Whether or not this monitor notifies when data stops reporting.

on_missing_data.description: Controls how groups or monitors are treated if an evaluation does not return any data points. The default option results in different behavior depending on the monitor query type. For monitors using `Count` queries, an empty monitor evaluation is treated as 0 and is compared to the threshold conditions. For monitors using any query type other than `Count`, for example `Gauge`, `Measure`, or `Rate`, the monitor shows the last known status. This option is only available for APM Trace Analytics, Audit Trail, CI, Error Tracking, Event, Logs, and RUM monitors. Valid values are: `show_no_data`, `show_and_notify_no_data`, `resolve`, and `default`.

renotify_interval.description: The number of minutes after the last notification before the monitor re-notifies on the current status.

renotify_occurrences.description: The number of re-notification messages that should be sent on the current status.

renotify_statuses.description: The types of statuses for which re-notification messages should be sent. Valid values are `alert`, `warn`, `no data`.

require_full_window.description: Whether or not the monitor needs a full window of data before it is evaluated.

tags.description: List of tags associated with the monitor.

tags_filter.description: A list of tags to limit the search. This filters on the monitor scope.

timeout_h.description: Number of hours of the monitor not reporting data before it automatically resolves from a triggered state.

type.description: Type of the monitor.

query.description: The query string to filter results based on SLO names. Some examples of queries include service:<service-name> and <slo-name>.

tags_query.description: Filter results based on a single SLO tag.

block.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about multiple SLOs for use in other resources.

filter_exclude_snapshot.description: Filter entities by excluding snapshotted entities.

filter_relation_type.description: Filter entities by relation type. Valid values are `RelationTypeOwns`, `RelationTypeOwnedBy`, `RelationTypeDependsOn`, `RelationTypeDependencyOf`, `RelationTypePartsOf`, `RelationTypeHasPart`, `RelationTypeOtherOwns`, `RelationTypeOtherOwnedBy`, `RelationTypeImplementedBy`, `RelationTypeImplements`.

block.description: Use this data source to list software catalog entities to use in other resources.

name.description: The synthetics global variable name to search for. Must only match one global variable. Must be all uppercase with underscores.

tags.description: A list of tags assigned to the Synthetics global variable.

block.description: Use this data source to retrieve a Datadog Synthetics global variable (to be used in Synthetics tests).

locations.description: A map of available Synthetics location IDs to names for Synthetics tests.

block.description: Use this data source to retrieve Datadog's Synthetics Locations (to be used in Synthetics tests).

name.description: The name of the synthetic test.

tags.description: A list of tags assigned to the synthetic test.

test_id.description: The synthetic test id or URL to search for

url.description: The start URL of the synthetic test.

block.description: Use this data source to retrieve a Datadog Synthetic Test.

enabled.description: Whether the ruleset is enabled.

position.description: The position of the ruleset in the pipeline.

name.description: The name of the rule.

destination_key.description: The destination key for the mapping.

if_not_exists.description: Whether to apply the mapping only if the destination key doesn't exist.

if_tag_exists.description: Behavior when the tag already exists. Valid values: `append` (append to the existing tag value), `replace` (replace existing tag value), `do_not_apply` (never apply if tag already exists). Valid values are `append`, `replace`, `do_not_apply`.

source_keys.description: The source keys for the mapping.

block.description: The mapping configuration for the rule.

case_insensitivity.description: Whether the query matching is case insensitive.

if_not_exists.description: Whether to apply the query only if the key doesn't exist.

if_tag_exists.description: Behavior when the tag already exists. Valid values: `append` (append to the existing tag value), `replace` (replace existing tag value), `do_not_apply` (never apply if tag already exists). Valid values are `append`, `replace`, `do_not_apply`.

block.description: The addition configuration for the query.

case_insensitivity.description: Whether the reference table lookup is case insensitive.

if_tag_exists.description: Behavior when the tag already exists. Valid values: `append` (append to the existing tag value), `replace` (replace existing tag value), `do_not_apply` (never apply if tag already exists). Valid values are `append`, `replace`, `do_not_apply`.

description.description: Free-form markdown description/content for the team's homepage.

filter_keyword.description: Search query. Can be team name, team handle, or email of team member.

link_count.description: The number of links belonging to the team.

name.description: The name of the team.

summary.description: A brief summary of the team, derived from the `description`.

user_count.description: The number of users belonging to the team.

block.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about an existing Datadog team.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the team hierarchy link was created.

provisioned_by.description: The provisioner of the team hierarchy link.

exact_match.description: When true, `filter_keyword` string is exact matched against the user's `email`, followed by `name`.

filter_keyword.description: Search query, can be user email or name.

block.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about existing Datadog team memberships.

filter_keyword.description: Search query. Can be team name, team handle, or email of team member.

filter_me.description: When true, only returns teams the current user belongs to.

description.description: Free-form markdown description/content for the team's homepage.

link_count.description: The number of links belonging to the team.

name.description: The name of the team.

summary.description: A brief summary of the team, derived from the `description`.

user_count.description: The number of users belonging to the team.

Use this data source to retrieve information about existing teams for use in other resources.

created_at.description: The time when the user was created (RFC3339 format).

disabled.description: Indicates whether the user is disabled.

exact_match.description: When true, `filter` string is exact matched against the user's `email`, followed by `name` attribute. Defaults to `false`.

exclude_service_accounts.description: When true, service accounts are excluded from the result. Defaults to `false`.

filter.description: Filter all users by the given string.

icon.description: The URL where the user's icon is located.

mfa_enabled.description: Indicates whether the user has enabled MFA.

modified_at.description: The time at which the user was last updated (RFC3339 format).

name.description: Name of the user.

service_account.description: Indicates whether the user is a service account.

verified.description: Indicates whether the user is verified.

block.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about an existing user to use it in an other resources.

filter.description: Filter all users by the given string.

filter_status.description: Filter on status attribute. Comma-separated list with possible values of Active, Pending, and Disabled.

block.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about existing users for use in other resources.

description.description: Description of the workflow.

name.description: Name of the workflow.

published.description: Set the workflow to published or unpublished. Workflows in an unpublished state are only executable through manual runs. Automatic triggers such as Schedule do not execute the workflow until it is published.

spec_json.description: The spec defines what the workflow does.

tags.description: Tags of the workflow.

block.description: This data source retrieves the definition of an existing Datadog workflow from Workflow Automation for use in other resources. This data source requires a [registered application key](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/DataDog/datadog/latest/docs/resources/app_key_registration).
